shlokas.original:
तप्यमाने तपो देवे देवा: सर्षिगणा: पुरा।
सेनापतिमभीप्सन्त: पितामहमुपागमन्।।1.37.1।।
shlokas.translation: In olden days when Iswara was engaged in austerities, the devatas together with the ascetics, approached Lord Brahma for an army general.

shlokas.original:
तत��ऽब्रुवन् सुरास्सर्वे भगवन्तं पितामहम्।
प्रणिपत्य सुरास्सर्वे सेन्द्रास्साग्निपुरोगमा:।।1.37.2।।
shlokas.translation: Then led by Indra and the Firegod all devatas approached Lord Brahma, the grandsire saluted him (with folded hands) and spoke.

shlokas.original:
यो न स्सेनापतिर्देव दत्तो भगवता पुरा।
तप: परममास्थाय तप्यते स्म सहोमया।।1.37.3।।
shlokas.translation: "O Lord in ancient times your lordship gave us an army general (at the time of  creation). He is engrossed in great penance along with Uma .

shlokas.original:
यदत्रानन्तरं कार्यं लोकानां हितकाम्यया।
संविधत्स्व विधानज्ञ त्वं हि न: परमा गति:।।1.37.4।।
shlokas.translation: "O Knower of law and order O Grandsire verily you are our supreme refuge committed to the welfare of the worlds. Tell us what we should do in this matter (now that we cannot procreate a general on account of Uma's curse)".

shlokas.original:
देवतानां वचश्श्रुत्वा सर्वलोकपितामह:।
सान्त्वयन्मधुरैर्वाक्यैस्त्रिदशानिदमब्रवीत्।।1.37.5।।
shlokas.translation: Hearing the words of the devatas, Brahma, the Grandsire of all the worlds consoled the devatas with sweet words, saying:

shlokas.original:
शैल पुत्र्या यदुक्तं तन्न प्रजास्यथ पत्निषु ।
तस्या वचनमक्लिष्टं सत्यमेतन्न संशय:।।1.37.6।।

इयमाकाशगा गङ्गा यस्यां पुत्रं हुताशन:।
जनयिष्यति देवानां सेनापतिमरिन्दमम्।।1.37.7।।
shlokas.translation: "Ganga keeps flowing in the celestial regions. Through Ganga the Firegod will beget a son who will be capable of destroying enemies. He will become the army general of the gods".

shlokas.original:
ज्येष्ठा शैलेन्द्रदुहिता मानयिष्यति तत्सुतम्।
उमायास्तद्बहुमतं भविष्यति न संशय:।।1.37.8।।
shlokas.translation: "Ganga, the eldest daughter of the king of the mountains, will tend that son of Agni. This will be totally acceptable to Uma. No doubt".

shlokas.original:
तच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं तस्य कृतार्था रघुनन्दन।
प्रणिपत्य सुरास्सर्वे पितामहमपूजयन्।।1.37.9���।
shlokas.translation: "O son of the Raghus having heard Brahma's words all the devatas considered themselves successful in their mission. They saluted him with folded hands and offered their worship.

shlokas.original:
ते गत्वा पर्वतं राम कैलासं धातुमण्डितम्।
अग्निं नियोजयामासु: पुत्रार्थं सर्वदेवता:।।1.37.10।।
shlokas.translation: All the devatas, went to mount Kailasa, adorned with minerals, and urged the Firegod for a son.

shlokas.original:
देवकार्यमिदं देव संविधत्स्व हुताशन।
शैलपुत्र्यां महातेजो गङ्गायां तेज उत्सृज।।1.37.11।।
shlokas.translation: 'O lustrous Firegod, accomplish this divine work. O god, possessed of mighty energy, release the vital fluid of Iswara in Ganga, daughter of the king of mountains.

shlokas.original:
देवतानां प्रतिज्ञाय गङ्गामभ्येत्य पावक:।
गर्भं धारय वै देवि देवतानामिदं प्रियम्।।1.37.12।।
shlokas.translation: "The Firegod gave his consent. The devatas approached Ganga and said, 'O goddess bear the energy of Siva (inyour world) since this cause is dear to the gods'.

shlokas.original:
तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा दिव्यं रूपमधारयत्।
दृष्ट्वा तन्महिमानं स समन्तादवकीर्यत।।1.37.13।।
shlokas.translation: "Hearing the words of the Firegod, Ganga assumed a divine form. Beholding her glory, the god of fire pervaded her from all sides.

shlokas.original:
समन्ततस्तदा देवीमभ्यषिञ्चत पावक:।
सर्वस्रोतांसि पूर्णानि गङ्गाया रघुनन्दन।।1.37.14।।
shlokas.translation: O Rama then, the Firegod discharged the vital fluid of Iswara, preserved in his body, which pervaded all sides of Ganga and all her streams were filled with (the fluid).

shlokas.original:
तमुवाच ततो गङ्गा सर्वदेवपुरोहितम्।
अशक्ता धारणे देव तव तेज स्समुद्धतम्।
दह्यमानाग्निना तेन सम्प्रव्यथितचेतना।।1.37.15।।
shlokas.translation: Ganga addressing the Firegod standing ahead of all the devatas said 'O god of fire, I am incapable of holding your everincreasing splendour. The fiery fluid is burning me. My consciousness is overwhelmed'.

shlokas.original:
अथाब्रवीदिदं गङ्गां सर्वदेवहुताशन:।
इह हैमवती पादे गर्भोऽयं सन्निवेश्यताम्।।1.37.16।।
shlokas.translation: Thereafter the Firegod who is the partaker of oblations offered to all deities said to Ganga 'Place this embryo on this slope of Mountain Himavan'.

shlokas.original:
श्रुत्वा त्वग्निवचो गङगा तं गर्भमतिभास्वरम्।
उत्ससर्ज महातेज स्स्रोतोभ्यो हि तदानघ ।।1.37.17।।
shlokas.translation: "O glorious, sinless one, (Rama) hearing the words of the Firegod, Ganga then expelled that resplendent embryo from her streams.

shlokas.original:
यदस्या निर्गतं तस्मात्तप्तजाम्बूनदप्रभम् ।
काञ्चनं धरणीं प्राप्तं हिरण्यममलं शुभम्।।1.37.18।।
shlokas.translation: The embryo that emerged from Ganga reached the earth. It resembled the lustre of molten gold. It furned into gold auspicious, bright and pure.

shlokas.original:
ताम्रं कार्ष्णायसं चैव तैक्ष्ण्यादेवाभ्यजायत।।1.37.19।।
मलं तस्याभवत्तत्र त्रपु सीसकमेव च।
तदेतद्धरणीं प्राप्य नानाधातुरवर्धत।।1.37.20।।
shlokas.translation: There copper and iron were produced from its acidity, zinc and lead from its residue. Various minerals were formed when that embryo reached the earth.

shlokas.original:
निक्षिप्तमात्रे गर्भे तु तेजोभिरभिरञ्जितम्।
सर्वं पर्वतसन्नद्धं सौवर्णमभवद्वनम्।।1.37.21।।
shlokas.translation: When the embryo was placed (in Ganga) it spread on all sides of the forest on the mountain. Irradiated with lustre the forest appeared golden.

shlokas.original:
जातरूपमिति ख्यातं तदा प्रभृति राघव।
सुवर्णं पुरुषव्याघ्र हुताशनसमप्रभम्।।1.37.22।।
तृणवृक्षलतागुल्मं सर्वं भवति काञ्चनम्।
shlokas.translation: O tiger among men, O scion of the Raghus Then onwards gold which is effulgent like fire, became wellknown as Jatarupa (pure form). The grass, shrubs, creepers and trees of that forest looked golden.

shlokas.original:
तं कुमारं ततो जातं सेन्द्रास्सह मरुद्गणा:।।1.37.23।।
क्षीरसंभावनार्थाय कृत्तिकास्समयोजन्।
shlokas.translation: Thereafter Kumara was born (out of Ganga's womb). The gods together with Indra arranged six nymphs (stars) to act  as nurses to suckle Kumara as their own offspring.

shlokas.original:
ता: क्षीरं जातमात्रस्य कृत्वा समयमुत्तमम्।।1.37.24।।
ददु: पुत्रोऽयमस्माकं सर्वासामिति निश्चिता:।
shlokas.translation: Those krittikas, having decided among themselves and having made an agreement with the gods saying 'This boy shall become a son to all of us' fed the newborn with their milk (breast milk).

shlokas.original:
ततस्तु देवता स्सर्वा: कार्तिकेय इति ब्रुवन्।।1.37.25।।
पुत्रस्त्रैलोक्यविख्यातो भविष्यति न संशय:।4
shlokas.translation: Thereafter, all the gods said, 'This son shall become renowned in the three worlds as Karttikeya, (son of krittikas). There is no doubt '.

shlokas.original:
तेषां तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा स्कन्नं गर्भपरिस्रवे।।1.37.26।।
स्नापयन् परया लक्ष्म्या दीप्यमानं यथानलम्।
shlokas.translation: Hearing the words of the gods, the krittikas bathed the baby that descended from the womb of Ganga resembling flaming fire and shining with great beauty.

shlokas.original:
स्कन्द इत्यब्रुवन् देवा: स्कन्नं गर्भपरिस्रवात्।।1.37.27।।
कार्तिकेयं महाभागं काकुत्स्थ ज्वलनोपमम्।
shlokas.translation: O scion of the Kakusthas, Karttikeya lustrous like flaming fire, was very fortunate Descended from the womb of the Ganga, he is named Skanda by the gods.

shlokas.original:
प्रादुर्भूतं तत: क्षीरं कृत्तिकानामनुत्तमम् ।।1.37.28।।
षण्णां षडाननो भूत्वा जग्राह स्तनजं पय:।
shlokas.translation: Then excellent milk surged in the breasts of krittikas. Assuming six mouths, he sucked the milk.

shlokas.original:
गृहीत्वा क्षीरमेकाह्ना सुकुमारवपुस्तदा।।1.37.29।।
अजयत्स्वेन वीर्येण दैत्यसेनागणान् विभु:।
shlokas.translation: Drinking milk just for a day and with a body still tender he could vanquish hosts of demons by his inborn prowess so capable be was

shlokas.original:
सुरसेनागणपतिं ततस्तमतुलद्युतिम्।।1.37.30।।
अभ्यषिञ्चन् सुरगणा स्समेत्याग्निपुरोगमा:।
shlokas.translation: With the Firegod in the forefront, all the gods assembled and installed Karttikeya, shining on unsurpassed brilliant, as commander of the gods forces.

shlokas.original:
एष ते राम गङ्गाया विस्तरोऽभिहितो मया।।1.37.31।।
कुमारसम्भवश्चैव धन्य: पुण्यस्तथैव च।
shlokas.translation: "O Rama this story of Ganga and that of the  birth of Kumara, who was fortunate and auspicious, has been related to you in detail.

shlokas.original:
भक्तश्च य: कार्तिकेये काकुत्स्थ भुवि मानवः।
आयुष्मान् पुत्रपौत्रैश्च स्कन्दसालोक्यतां व्रजेत्।।1.37.32।।
shlokas.translation: O son of the Kakusthas whosoever reveres Kartikeya on this earth with devotion and faith, shall have a long life, sons and grandsons and after death will reach the world of  Skanda". इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये बालकाण्डेण्डे सप्तत्रिंशस्सर्ग:।। Thus ends the thirtyseventh sarga of Balakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
